name: Node3d<T>
summary: Basic node in a Tree3d structure. Nodes in tree structures maintain a local region and either a list of content indices or a list of up to 8 child nodes.

constructors.name: Node3d<T>(Coordinates3d<T>, BoundingBox)
constructors.summary: Create a new spatial tree root.

params.name: region
params.doc: Bounding box for this node. The region should be big enough to contain all items you're going to insert.

constructors.name: Node3d<T>(Coordinates3d<T>, BoundingBox, Int32)
constructors.signature: public Node3d(Coordinates3d<T> converter, BoundingBox region, int limit)
constructors.summary: Create a new spatial tree root.

params.name: region
params.doc: Bounding box for this node. The region should be big enough to contain all items you're going to insert.

methods.name: Add
methods.summary: Insert another item into the tree. The item should be within the region of this node.

methods.name: AddRange
methods.summary: Insert a collection of items into the tree. The items should all be within the region of this node.

methods.name: CollapseNodes
methods.summary: Collapse the (sub)tree rooted at this node. Collapsing happens when a node only has a single child, in which case the child usurps the position previously held by the parent. Do not collapse a (sub)tree if you still plan to add items later.

returns.doc: The new root for this (sub)tree. May well be an instance of this node if it cannot be collapsed.
The index of the furtest item or null if no furtest item could be found.
The index of the furtest item or null if no furtest item could be found.

returns.doc: The index of the furtest item or null if no furtest item could be found.

methods.name: FurthestItem(T, Double, Double)
methods.signature: public Index3d<T> FurthestItem(T locus, double minimumDistance, double maximumDistance)

params.name: minimumDistance
params.doc: Minimum distance for furtest item. Items closer than minimumDistance will be ignored.

params.name: maximumDistance
params.doc: Maximum distance for furtest item. Items further than maximumDistance will be ignored.

returns.doc: The index of the furtest item or null if no furtest item could be found.

returns.doc: The index of the furtest item or null if no furtest item could be found.

params.doc: Maximum distance for nearest items. Items further than maximumDistance will be ignored.

methods.name: OptimizeTree
methods.summary: Optimize this tree for fast searches. Do not call this method if you still plan to add items in the future. You can no longer modify this tree once it has been optimized.

returns.doc: The new root of the optimized tree. If the root could not be optimized an instance of this node will be returned.

methods.name: Remove(Index3d<T>)
methods.summary: Remove the item with the given index from the entire tree. It doesn't matter on which node you call this function, it is a tree-wide operation.

methods.name: Remove(Int32)
methods.summary: Remove the item with the given index from the entire tree. It doesn't matter on which node you call this function, it is a tree-wide operation.

methods.name: ShrinkRegions
methods.summary: Shrink the region for this node and all child nodes. Do not use this method if you intend to add more items later as it creates spatial gaps in the tree structure. You can shrink nodes if you're done adding items and want to start searching the tree.

methods.name: SubTree
methods.summary: Gets an iterator for all nodes in this (sub)tree.

params.name: omitStructuralNodes
params.doc: If true, nodes that contain other nodes will be skipped. Only nodes containing items will be included.

methods.name: TrimExcess
methods.summary: Trim the excess space on all index lists. You can call this method to reduce memory usage. It will not modify the tree in any functional way.

properties.name: Center
properties.summary: Gets the center of the spatial region of this node. If the node contains no children the center is always in the middle of the Region. If the node does contain child-nodes, the center may be anywhere within the region.

properties.name: ChildCount
properties.summary: Gets the number of defined child nodes. Leaf nodes have no children. Root and twig nodes can have anywhere between 1 and 8 children.

properties.name: ChildNode
properties.summary: Gets the child node at the given index.

properties.name: ContentAverage
properties.summary: Returns the average coordinate of all items in this node. If this node does not contain any items, Point3d.Unset is returned.

properties.name: ContentBoundingBox
properties.summary: Returns the boundingbox of all items in this node. If this node does not contain any items, BoundingBox.Empty is returned.

properties.name: IndicesLocal
properties.summary: Gets the list of item indices that are contained within this node.

properties.name: IndicesRecursive
properties.summary: Gets the list of item indices that are contained within this node and any child nodes.

properties.name: IsLeaf
properties.summary: Gets whether this node is a leaf node. Leaf nodes have no child nodes.

properties.name: IsMutable
properties.summary: Gets whether this tree is mutable. You can only add items to mutable trees. We don't recommend removing items from unmutable trees, though that shouldn't necessarily lead to problems. Trees become unmutable after a call to ShrinkRegions(), CollapseNodes() or OptimizeTree().

properties.name: IsRoot
properties.summary: Gets whether this node is a root node. Root nodes have no parent node and depth zero.

properties.name: IsTwig
properties.summary: Gets whether this node is a twig node. Twig nodes have both parents and at least one child.

properties.name: ItemCount
properties.summary: Gets the total number of items stored directly in this node.

properties.name: ItemsGlobal
properties.summary: Gets the list of all items stored inside this entire tree. Do not modify this collection unless you know what you are doing.

properties.name: ItemsLocal
properties.summary: Gets a list of all the items stored directly in this node. This list is constructed every time you access this property, so keep it down to a minimum.

properties.name: Limit
properties.summary: Gets the subdivision limit for this tree. This limit can only be set once when you create a new tree. It is fixed forever after.

properties.name: MemoryConsumption
properties.summary: Gets the estimated memory consumption of the (sub)tree structure. Items inside the global list are not included in this estimate.

properties.name: NextNode
properties.summary: Gets the logical neighbour to the right of this node. There is no spatial relationships between logical neighbours, this is purely an iteration aid.

properties.name: NodeDepth
properties.summary: Gets the recursive depth of this node. The tree root is at depth zero. The first subdivision is at depth one, and so on and so forth.

properties.name: ParentNode
properties.summary: Gets the immediate parent of this node. Root nodes have no parent.

properties.name: Region
properties.summary: Gets the spatial region of this node.

properties.name: RootNode
properties.summary: Gets the ultimate root node for this tree.

properties.name: WeightedSubdivision
properties.summary: Gets or sets whether subdivision is weighted based on content averages. Setting this value will only affect future subdivisions, not existing ones.
